evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce Acme Corp
evals.expected_output: Pushes SE Status to Salesforce — no deal context pull, just push
expectations: Checks sf CLI is installed and authenticated
Reads config from ~/.claude/skills/ld-config.md and extracts all required values
Account file read from {config.company_folder}/Accounts/Acme Corp/Acme Corp.md
Auto-correct check performed for swapped salesforce_account/salesforce_opportunity URLs
Opportunity ID extracted from salesforce_opportunity frontmatter field
No deal context fields queried (push mode only pushes)
Salesforce Updates code block content extracted (between triple backticks)
Content written to temp file /tmp/sf_update.txt using Write tool
Access token obtained via sf org display --target-org {config.salesforce_username} --json

JSON payload built with HTML formatting (newlines to
, bold MEDDPICC/TECHMAPS/TECH STACK/DEAL HISTORY headers)
REST API PATCH to /services/data/v62.0/sobjects/Opportunity/{id} with {config.salesforce_se_status_field} field
HTTP 204 interpreted as success
Temp files /tmp/sf_update.txt and /tmp/sf_update.json cleaned up after update
Ledger.md checked for (status) entry and removed if found
Daily note checkbox for /ld-salesforce Acme Corp marked complete with [completion:: YYYY-MM-DD]
Success report includes Opportunity ID, label, and first 5 lines of content preview

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce NonExistentAccount
evals.expected_output: Reports error when account file doesn't exist
expectations: Error reported: account file not found
No REST API calls executed
No temp files created

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce AccountWithNoOpps
evals.expected_output: Warns user when no opportunity URLs are in frontmatter
expectations: Account file read successfully
No salesforce_opportunity fields found or all empty
No push attempted
Warning message tells user to add salesforce_opportunity field

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce scan Initech
evals.expected_output: Imports all opportunities with historical deal context, creates files, builds Dataview table
expectations: Checks sf CLI is installed and authenticated
Mode detected as scan from 'scan' prefix in arguments
Account file read from {config.company_folder}/Accounts/Initech/Initech.md
Auto-correct check performed for swapped salesforce_account/salesforce_opportunity URLs
Salesforce Account ID extracted from salesforce_account frontmatter field
Access token obtained via sf org display
SOQL queries executed for Opportunities, OpportunityFieldHistory, Events, and Notes
Raw query results saved to temp JSON file for subagent consumption
Subagents launched in parallel to process opportunity history (grouped by era: recent/older/historical)
Recent opportunities (last 12 months) get individual subagents with detailed milestone extraction
Older opportunities (1-3 years) batched into one subagent with key milestones only
Historical opportunities (3+ years) batched into one subagent with one-line summaries
opportunities/ subdirectory created under account folder
Individual opportunity .md files created with correct frontmatter (account, opportunity_name, stage, amount, close_date, type, status, salesforce_url, created_date)
Filenames sanitized: invalid characters (/ \ : * ? " < > |) replaced with -
Duplicate opportunity names disambiguated with close date or ID suffix
opportunities.base file created with Dataview table configuration (table view, sorted by close_date desc)
Opportunity History section added to account file with ![[opportunities.base]] embed
Salesforce Deal History narrative added under Opportunity History with chronological milestones
Deal Ledger updated with Salesforce History section at bottom
Overall pattern analysis included (e.g., noting all closed-lost but existing customer)
salesforce_account field preserved or set in frontmatter
Links line updated to include Salesforce Account link
Temp files cleaned up
Summary table output with opportunity count, open/won/lost breakdown, and deal history summary

evals.expected_output: Falls back to extracting Account ID from an existing opportunity URL when salesforce_account is missing
expectations: Mode detected as scan
Account file read successfully
No salesforce_account field found in frontmatter
Falls back to salesforce_opportunity field to get an Opportunity ID
REST API call to /sobjects/Opportunity/{opp_id}?fields=AccountId to retrieve Account ID
Account ID used for SOQL queries
Proceeds with normal scan flow after obtaining Account ID

expectations: Mode detected as scan

No salesforce_account field found

Prompts user for Salesforce Account URL
No SOQL query executed without Account ID

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce scan Initech
evals.expected_output: Handles account with all closed opportunities correctly — notes discrepancy if account is an existing customer
expectations: All opportunities identified as closed (IsClosed = true)
Status correctly derived: Closed Won for IsWon=true, Closed Lost for IsWon=false
Opportunity files created for all closed opportunities
salesforce_opportunity fields left empty (no open opportunities to link)
Deal history notes that all opps are closed-lost but account appears to be existing customer
Summary reports 0 open opportunities

expectations: First open opportunity set as salesforce_opportunity
Additional open opportunities set as salesforce_opportunity_{type} (e.g., salesforce_opportunity_renewal)
Type suffix derived from Opportunity Type field, lowercased
Existing salesforce_opportunity fields for open opps preserved
Summary lists all open opportunities linked in frontmatter

evals.expected_output: Granularity scales with recency — recent opps get detailed timelines, old opps get one-liners
expectations: Opportunities grouped by era for subagent processing
Recent opportunity subagents extract detailed stage-by-stage timeline
Older opportunity subagents produce 2-4 bullet point summaries
Historical opportunity subagents produce single sentence summaries
Salesforce Deal History in account file organized by era with appropriate detail levels

expectations: Duplicate names detected during file creation

Frontmatter opportunity_name preserves the original Salesforce name

New MEDDPICC info from Salesforce appended with (from Salesforce) attribution
Existing manually-written MEDDPICC content NOT overwritten
Ledger updated if deal has progressed since last entry, format: - M/D {initials} (from SF): {summary}
Stale fields flagged (stage, close date, ARR, forecast changes)
AE/CSM activity summarized — milestones included, routine outreach filtered out

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce Acme Corp
evals.expected_output: Validates that the Salesforce Updates section has correct structure: latest entry at top, older entries in DEAL HISTORY
expectations: Salesforce Updates code block starts with the LATEST meeting entry (most recent date) as the first line
Only ONE entry appears above the MEDDPICC section (the most recent)
MEDDPICC section follows immediately after the latest entry, with fields on separate lines (Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, etc.)
TECHMAPS section follows MEDDPICC, with fields on separate lines
TECH STACK is a single comma-separated line
DEAL HISTORY section is at the bottom, containing the SECOND most recent entry and all older entries
DEAL HISTORY entries are ordered newest first
The latest entry date is NOT duplicated in DEAL HISTORY
Date format uses M/D (no leading zeros, no year) for current year, M/D/YY for prior years
All entries use {config.initials} format for attribution
Content pushed to Salesforce matches the code block content exactly

Opportunity classified as 'Mine' when SE field matches current user's ID
Opportunity classified as 'Unmapped' when all SE fields are null/empty
Opportunity classified as 'Another SE's' when SE field is set to a different user
Skipped opportunities reported with other SE's name and opp ID
Push proceeds only for eligible (mine + unmapped) opportunities
Skipped opps do NOT receive the REST API PATCH call

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce Acme Corp (Deal Health assessed as part of weekly review, pushed via salesforce_deal_health_field)
evals.expected_output: Deal Health color pushed to correct Salesforce field based on deal state signals
expectations: Config value {config.salesforce_deal_health_field} (SE_Opportunity_Health__c) read from ld-config.md

Deal health assessed using holistic signals: days since last meeting, champion engagement, MEDDPICC completeness, stage velocity, close date movement, stakeholder breadth, technical validation status, competitive pressure, next steps clarity
Green assigned when: most signals positive, champion engaged, timeline on track
Yellow assigned when: 1-2 risk signals (slowing momentum, MEDDPICC gap, pushed close date) but deal still viable
Red assigned when: multiple risk signals (champion dark, 30+ day stall, single-threaded, competitor winning)
One-line justification (max ~15 words) generated for the score
REST API PATCH to {config.salesforce_deal_health_field} with value Green, Yellow, or Red
Ledger status entry includes health color emoji and justification

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce Acme Corp (Deal Health: champion gone dark 30+ days, single-threaded)

expectations: Multiple red signals detected: no contact 30+ days, single-threaded, champion unresponsive
Deal health scored as Red

Red health pushed to SE_Opportunity_Health__c field
Ledger entry uses red emoji indicator

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce Acme Corp (Deal Health: active POV, close date pushed once, one MEDDPICC gap)
evals.expected_output: Yellow deal health assigned — viable but needs attention
expectations: Mixed signals detected: POV active (positive), close date pushed once (negative), MEDDPICC gap (negative)
Deal health scored as Yellow (not Red because deal is still progressing)
Justification reflects specific risk: e.g., 'Close date pushed to 4/30. Waiting on budget approval.'
Yellow health pushed to SE_Opportunity_Health__c field

evals.prompt: /ld-salesforce Acme Corp (Deal Health: POV complete, multi-threaded, close date on track)

expectations: All major signals positive: POV complete, multiple contacts, timeline on track, champion engaged
